differential_diagnoses: Sepsis due to occult bacterial infection
Severe viral hepatitis (e.g., Hepatitis A or E)
next_steps: Send diagnostic testing for Leptospirosis including PCR (blood/urine) and MAT (microscopic agglutination test)
Obtain a peripheral blood smear for detection of intraerythrocytic organisms (Babesia species)
Perform urinalysis and urine microscopy for markers of tubular injury and evaluation for leptospires
Measure serum creatinine kinase, lactate dehydrogenase, and haptoglobin to evaluate for rhabdomyolysis and hemolysis
Send viral hepatitis panel including IgM anti-HAV and anti-HEV
Send serum for Hantavirus serology and evaluate for viral RNA if available
Continue broad-spectrum antibiotic coverage (doxycycline, vancomycin, and cefepime) while monitoring for signs of clinical improvement
